articles.title: Program Details
articles.description: Learn about the 24-week program structure, family enrollment options, and multidisciplinary support available.

articles.title: Treatment Outcomes
articles.description: Understand expected outcomes, limitations, risks, and how to prevent relapse after completing therapy.

articles.title: Specific Treatment Concerns
articles.description: Information about TMJ, speech therapy, orthodontics, allergies, and ankyloglossia (tongue-tie) treatment.

articles.title: Introduction to Myofunctional Therapy
articles.description: Learn what myofunctional therapy is, how it works, and who can benefit from this evidence-based approach to improving oral function.

articles.title: The Four Goals of Myofunctional Therapy
articles.description: Discover the four foundational goals: nasal breathing, lip seal, proper chewing/swallowing, and correct tongue posture.

articles.title: Expectations for Myofunctional Therapy
articles.description: Understand the time commitment, what results to expect, and how to get the most out of your therapy program.

articles.title: Structural Changes Caused by Myofunctional Disorders
articles.description: Explore how myofunctional disorders affect facial development, palate shape, dental alignment, and airway structure.

articles.title: Health/Systemic Effects Associated with Myofunctional Disorders
articles.description: Learn about the wide-ranging health impacts including sleep quality, cardiovascular health, and cognitive function.

articles.title: How Myofunctional Therapy Improves Sleep Apnea
articles.description: Understand how strengthening oral muscles and improving tongue posture can reduce sleep apnea symptoms.

articles.title: Signs of Sleep-Disordered Breathing in Children
articles.description: Recognize the warning signs of sleep-disordered breathing in children, from snoring to behavioral changes.

articles.title: Causes of Myofunctional Disorders During Infancy
articles.description: Discover how feeding practices, pacifier use, and other infant factors can contribute to myofunctional disorders.

articles.title: Myofunctional Therapy Program Specifics
articles.description: Details about our 24-week program structure, session format, and what to expect each week.

articles.title: Compensations to Limit (with photos)
articles.description: Visual guide to common compensatory patterns like neck tension and jaw movement that should be avoided.

articles.title: Frenectomy Pathway (Readiness & Timeline)
articles.description: Information about tongue-tie release, when it's recommended, and how to prepare for the procedure.

articles.title: Your Myofunctional Therapy Kit
articles.description: Overview of the supplies included in your therapy kit and how each item is used during exercises.
